headline: Autism Diagnosis Wait Time Reaches 18 Months at Public Facilities
context: Children suspected of autism spectrum disorder face 18-month waits for diagnostic assessment at government hospitals.

cards.big: Autism diagnostic wait averages 18 months at public hospitals delaying crucial early intervention
cards.sub: Early intervention before age 3 produces 50% better developmental outcomes

cards.big: Malaysia has 38 developmental pediatricians for estimated 300,000 autistic individuals
cards.sub: Specialist scarcity creates bottleneck in assessment and diagnosis pathways

cards.big: Private autism diagnosis costs RM3,000-5,000 making it inaccessible for B40 families
cards.sub: Wealthy families bypass public queues while poor children wait and lose critical development time

cards.big: UK NHS introduced community-based autism assessment by trained GPs reducing wait to 3 months
cards.sub: Task-shifting diagnosis from specialists to trained primary care enabled faster assessment

cards.big: Every month a child waits for autism diagnosis is a month of lost intervention opportunity
Should trained primary care doctors be authorized to make autism diagnoses to reduce wait times
